svg.text: The Dhamma that is beneficial in the beginning, in the middle, and in the end
the meeting of the three is contact
with contact as condition · phassapaccayā vedanā
each being pleasant, painful, or neither-painful-nor-pleasant
sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā
with felt experience as condition · vedanāpaccayā taṇhā
arise with felt experience as condition

g.text: “The arising and passing away of the
“The arising and passing away of the

svg.text: “Positing a self in any of these six sets of six is not tenable,
as otherwise, one would have to conclude: ‘My self arises and passes away.’”
When touched by a PLEASANT felt experience
one delights in it (abhinandati) · welcomes it (abhivadati)
· remains holding tightly to it (ajjhosāya tiṭṭhati)
underlying tendency to desire lies dormant in them
When touched by a PAINFUL felt experience
underlying tendency to aversion lies dormant in them
When touched by a NEUTRAL felt experience
one does not truly understand (yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti)
the arising, passing away, gratification, drawback,
underlying tendency to ignorance lies dormant in them
“By abandoning ignorance and arousing true knowledge,
that one shall make an end of suffering in the here and now — this is possible.”
avijjaṁ pahāya vijjaṁ uppādetvā diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhassantakaro bhavissatīti — ṭhānametaṁ vijjati
